title: Define per-work record + per-jurisdiction rights-verification format
context: Every narrated work requires a structured per-work record capturing source provenance, rights verification, attribution, consent references, audio metadata, alignment metadata, and publication status. Without a stable, versioned record format, the pipeline cannot enforce the hard rights gate (per-jurisdiction PD or open license), emit auditable decision logs, or track a deed from narration to published-live status. This format is a prerequisite for all pipeline and narration tasks.
objective: Define and publish v1 of the per-work narration record schema and the per-jurisdiction rights-verification record format that all pipeline and narration tasks will populate.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-work record captures: workId, chapterId, sourceUrl, sourceCollection, edition, author (+ translator if any), rights object (status, license, jurisdictionsVerified[], evidenceUrl), attribution string, narrationType (human | synthetic), speakerRef (reference only, no raw PII), audio metadata (master/opus/mp3 paths, durationSec, LUFS, truePeakDb, noiseFloorDb), pronunciationLexicon entries, alignment metadata (smilPath, vttPath, jsonPath, medianOnsetErrMs, segmentsCorrected), qaStatus, qaScores (fidelity, pronunciation, audioQuality, alignment), upstreamRef, publishStatus, label (human-narrated | AI-synthesized), and provenance.

Rights-verification sub-record requires: PD status confirmed per jurisdiction (not assumed globally), license id, evidence URL, retrieval date, and attribution — with 'unknown / PD-US-only without scoping / ambiguous / all-rights-reserved' all mapping to 'excluded'.
Translations and introductions are flagged as needing separate rights checks in the schema.
Schema is published as a versioned JSON Schema (or equivalent) with a worked example for a PD work and a CC-BY work.
No raw PII or speaker identity is stored in public record fields; speakerRef is a reference to the separately-stored consent record.
Licensed CC0-1.0 (public-domain schema for maximum reuse).
resources: PLAN.md (per-work narration record data model, data / licensing / compliance sections)
EPUB 3 Media Overlays specification (SMIL timing fields)
output: A versioned per-work record schema file (schema/work-record.schema.json or equivalent) and a companion rights-verification format document, CC0-1.0, with worked examples for at least one PD and one CC-BY source.
